meta.title: Batch 4 Download Manifest — Critical Corpus Gaps
meta.purpose: Identify and download documents missing from the training corpus. Priority: CRITICAL first, then HIGH. These are the gaps between what exists in training/stage-1/ and what is needed for complete regulatory AI coverage.

priority_1_critical.description: Must have — these are either #1 inspection findings globally or cover mandatory post-marketing requirements

ema_gvp_module_vi.title: EMA GVP Module VI — Management and Reporting of Adverse Reactions to Medicinal Products
ema_gvp_module_vi.why_critical: Core pharmacovigilance — defines Individual Case Safety Report (ICSR) standards, expedited reporting (15-day rule), aggregate reports (PSURs/PBRERs), EudraVigilance obligations. Every EU MA holder must comply. Covers same ground as E2D(R1) but with EU-specific operational rules.

ema_gvp_module_xii.title: EMA GVP Module XII — Signal Management
ema_gvp_module_xii.why_critical: Defines signal detection, validation, analysis, and regulatory action obligations. EMA PRAC signal management process. Applies to all MA holders in EU. Directly relevant to post-market safety surveillance AI.

pics_pi_032.title: PIC/S PI 032 — Good Practices for Data Management and Integrity in Regulated GMP/GDP Environments
pics_pi_032.why_critical: #1 GMP inspection finding globally (2015–2025). Defines data integrity (ALCOA+), audit trails, electronic records, hybrid systems, CSV/CDS requirements. Applies to all GMP-regulated manufacturing. Any AI advising on CMC or manufacturing must know this.
pics_pi_032.download_note: PIC/S documents require registration on picscheme.org but are publicly available. Alternate: FDA Data Integrity Guidance (2018) covers much of the same ground and is available at FDA.gov — download both.

pics_pi_041.title: PIC/S PI 041 — Good Manufacturing Practice for Advanced Therapy Medicinal Products
pics_pi_041.why_critical: ATMP GMP is the fastest-growing regulatory frontier (gene therapy, cell therapy, tissue engineering). PI 041 complements EU Annex 2 (ATMP) and fills gaps in ICH Q7/Q10 for ATMPs. Already have gap-fill docs but missing the primary GMP standard.
pics_pi_041.download_note: Also download EMA/CAT GMP Annex for ATMPs if not already present.

fda_data_integrity_2018.title: FDA Guidance for Industry: Data Integrity and Compliance with Drug CGMP (2018)
fda_data_integrity_2018.why_critical: FDA's definitive data integrity guidance covering ALCOA, audit trails, electronic records (21 CFR Part 11), Computerized System Validation. Complements PIC/S PI 032 for FDA-regulated facilities.

priority_2_high.description: Strongly recommended — cover high-traffic regulatory areas or mandatory EU/PMDA-specific requirements

ema_gvp_module_x.title: EMA GVP Module X — Additional Monitoring
ema_gvp_module_x.why_high: Defines black triangle/additional monitoring obligations for new MA holders. Mandatory for all EU approvals since 2013. Governs risk minimisation measures.

ema_gvp_module_iii.title: EMA GVP Module III — Pharmacovigilance Inspections

ema_gvp_module_iv.title: EMA GVP Module IV — Pharmacovigilance Audits

ema_gvp_module_xi.title: EMA GVP Module XI — PSURs / PBRERs
ema_gvp_module_xi.why_high: Periodic Safety Update Reports / Periodic Benefit-Risk Evaluation Reports — mandatory 6-monthly and annual regulatory submissions. One of the highest-workload post-approval obligations.

pmda_ddi_guidance.title: PMDA Guidance on Drug Interaction Studies for New Drug Applications
pmda_ddi_guidance.why_high: PMDA DDI requirements differ from ICH M12 in key areas (CYP2C19 weighting, transporter panel, clinical study triggers). Already have PMDA biologics/ethnics/stability but missing the DDI guidance.

pmda_oncology_guidance.title: PMDA Guidance on Clinical Evaluation of Anti-Cancer Drugs
pmda_oncology_guidance.why_high: Japan is a major oncology market. PMDA has specific requirements for oncology trial design, basket/umbrella trial acceptance, and post-approval requirements that differ from FDA/EMA.

fda_ind_regulations_full.title: 21 CFR Part 312 — IND Regulations (complete annotated)
fda_ind_regulations_full.why_high: Core IND regulations. Already have title21_vol5_parts300-499 but Part 312 should be extracted standalone for rapid reference. Covers IND application content, safety reporting, protocol amendments.
fda_ind_regulations_full.note: Already in corpus as part of title21_vol5 — may just need targeted extraction, not re-download

fda_505b2_guidance.title: FDA Guidance for Industry: Applications Covered by Section 505(b)(2) (1999, updated)
fda_505b2_guidance.why_high: 505(b)(2) pathway governs NDAs that rely on studies not conducted by the applicant — critical for reformulations, new dosage forms, new indications. Major pathway for novel drug development.

fda_rems_guidance.title: FDA Guidance: Format and Content of Proposed Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategies (REMS)
fda_rems_guidance.why_high: REMS requirements are mandated for high-risk drugs. Covers REMS elements (MedGuides, communication plans, ETASU), submission content, and FDA review process.

priority_3_medium.description: Important but not blocking — gaps that matter for complete coverage of niche areas

ich_guidelines_remaining.note: ~43 ICH guidelines still unextracted after batch 1+2. See batch 3 agents covering E3/E4/E7/E14/E17 and S1-S8/Q10/Q11. Remaining after batch 3 likely include: E5(R1), E6(R2), E8(R1), E9(R1), Q1A-Q1F, Q4B annexes, Q6A, Q6B, Q8(R2), S2(R1), S3A, S3B, S4, S5(R3), S8.
ich_guidelines_remaining.approach: Launch batch 4 extraction agents after batch 3 completes

eu_clinical_trials_regulation.title: EU Clinical Trials Regulation (EU) No 536/2014 (CTR)
eu_clinical_trials_regulation.why_medium: EU CTR replaced the EU Clinical Trials Directive in 2022. CTIS portal mandatory for all new EU trials. Key changes: single submission for multi-MS trials, harmonized timelines, transparency obligations.

eu_gvp_product_info.title: EMA Guideline on Smpc, Labelling and Package Leaflet
eu_gvp_product_info.why_medium: EU-specific labeling requirements (SmPC = EU equivalent of FDA Prescribing Information). Governs what must appear in summary of product characteristics. Different structure from FDA labeling PLR format.

health_canada_guidance_package.title: Health Canada: Guidance for Industry — Quality of Biotechnological Products + Clinical Trial Applications
health_canada_guidance_package.why_medium: Canada follows ICH closely but has specific HC-only requirements (Notice of Compliance with Conditions, Priority Review policy, Therapeutic Products Directorate SOPs).
health_canada_guidance_package.note: Check training/stage-1/health-canada/ for existing docs first

download_instructions.ema_gvp_base_url: Search EMA website for 'GVP Module [number]' — documents are freely available at ema.europa.eu
download_instructions.pics_base_url: https://picscheme.org — free registration required for PI documents
download_instructions.save_structure: All documents should be saved to appropriate subdirectory under training/stage-1/ and then extraction agents should be run on them
download_instructions.immediate_action: CRITICAL items (EMA GVP VI, XII; PIC/S PI 032, PI 041; FDA Data Integrity) should be downloaded before Batch 4 extraction agents are launched

batch_4_extraction_plan.description: After downloads complete, run extraction agents in parallel

proposed_agents.name: ema_gvp_vi_xii
proposed_agents.focus: ICSR reporting, expedited timelines, signal management, PRAC process

proposed_agents.name: pics_data_integrity
proposed_agents.focus: ALCOA+, audit trails, hybrid systems, CSV, electronic records
